input.headline: Erasmus open to Nienaber return for Rugby World Cup

raw.headline: Erasmus open to Nienaber return for Rugby World Cup

winner: Erasmus said he'd never play rugby again after that tackle—now he's back for the World Cup and somehow still hasn’t learned how to hold a scrum.

candidates.text: Why did Nienaber bring a ladder to the Erasmus meeting? Because he wanted to *step up* his game for the Rugby World Cup!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'step up' and rugby context are clever and specific, with good surprise factor and natural English."}

candidates.text: I told him if he doesn’t sign that deal with Erasmus, they’ll have to start calling him "Nienaber-ius.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'Nienaber-ius,' but it's a bit forced and lacks clarity for those unfamiliar with the context of Erasmus and Nienaber in rugby."}

candidates.text: Why did Nienaber get kicked off the Erasmus program? Because he was too busy trying out for the Rugby World Cup—turns out, you can't be a study abroad student and a try-scoring machine at the same time!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative and ties the headline elements together, but it relies on a weak pun ('try-scoring machine' vs 'study abroad student') and assumes knowledge not directly stated in the headline."}

candidates.text: Erasmus said he'd never play rugby again after that tackle—now he's back for the World Cup and somehow still hasn’t learned how to hold a scrum.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific to the headline, uses a surprising twist with the scrum detail, and has a touch of humor. However, it's slightly generic in its setup and could be more concise."}

candidates.text: Sure, let him back—we’re not exactly fielding a team of legends here.
